content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic Daily Update (Apr 8, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development activity for April 8, 2026, focused on initiating updates to the Anthropic plugin to improve model configuration and compatibility with the latest AI SDK standards.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*No technical developments were completed during this period.*

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#15](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic/pull/15): Proposes updates for Opus 4.x, including setting temperature to 1, implementing an output cap, and updating to `ai-sdk` v6 maxOutputTokens naming conventions.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*None.*

## NEW ISSUES
*None.*

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   [#6514](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic/issues/6514) Inter-Agent Coordination: 
    *   The discussion continues regarding cross-instance communication and agent identity. 
    *   Recent contributions focus on the "cold-start" problem for agent trust. 
    *   Community members proposed using on-chain credentials (via `eliza-plugin-insumer`) and append-only on-chain attestation sequences (WTRMRK) to establish persistent behavioral history and verifiable trust anchors between independent Eliza instances. 
    *   Participants emphasized that these methods allow agents to verify peer reliability and capability without requiring human mediation.
